blocks.content: Shirone grasped Amy's hand firmly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy, you're strong. Stronger than anyone I know. You'll find a solution. You always have.
Amy turned to look at Shirone.
The moment their eyes met, it felt like electricity coursed through her body.
The moonlit scenery, the sounds echoing off the walls, and the feel of Shirone's hand made her heart race.
It was as if the discomfort from just meeting his gaze earlier had been a lie, and now, courage surged within both of them.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: 'Shirone...'
Amy had to make a choice.
She was given another year, a time that felt like hell.
Having already experienced one failure, her focus had to be solely on mastering magic.
But for the first time in her life, she wanted to rely on someone, to lean on someone.
She didn't have the strength or confidence to overcome this alone.
Even in this moment of silence, Shirone looked at her with unwavering eyes.
She had to cross the point of no return.
If she turned away now, Shirone would leave her side.
To keep Shirone, she had to accept him.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy thought she had made up her mind. But at the same time, new hesitation surged within her.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Selfish thoughts.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Was it too much to ask for him to stay by her side, even if she didn't choose anything, even if she pretended not to know?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: 'Just one more year... Can't you give me just one more year? So I can make a careful decision, and until then, stay with me like this?'
Amy slowly withdrew her hand.
If she thought this would disappoint him, she wouldn't have been able to pull away.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy felt a sneer rise within her.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: 'What? You said you made up your mind? Liar.'
She knew the truth.
Shirone would wait for her.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Even if it was selfish, she couldn't help it. She couldn't give up either the graduation exam or Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Thank you, Shirone. I'm glad you're here.
Amy wanted to hide somewhere after saying that.
The words she had carefully chosen felt so insincere.
But Shirone smiled gently, just as she had hoped.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Me too. Let's do our best.
Shirone squeezed Amy's shoulder firmly, then went back to his spot and laid out his sleeping bag on the floor.
Amy felt relieved.
It was as if they had returned to a time before the recent events.
But the look in Shirone's eyes, captured by her Red Eyes, reminded her that it was all real.
How long would he wait for her?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Feelings of self-contempt and guilt towards Shirone tangled within Amy's eyes.
Past midnight.
Jokre and his friends were quite drunk.
Still unsatisfied, they bought a large bottle of whiskey and snacks from a shop and settled in an open space near the castle walls.
Jokre opened the strong whiskey and poured it into the glasses in front of Rudvans and Vivian.
Finally, he filled his own glass and drank it down without even toasting.

blocks.speaker: Rudvans
blocks.content: Ugh, it's really strong. What are you doing? Aren't you going to drink?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Aren't you drinking too much? What about tomorrow?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Ugh, if I feel sick, I'll just stay in the inn.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Ugh, it's really strong. What are you doing? Aren't you going to drink?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: 'Aren't you drinking too much? What about tomorrow?'

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Ugh, if I feel sick, I'll just stay in the inn.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Sigh, graduating isn't that great. I thought I'd have a lot of fun once I graduated. But no guys are interested.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Aren't we guys too?

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Oh, please. Do you think you can hit on me with that face?

blocks.speaker: Rudvans
blocks.content: Haha! Vivian, you're not that pretty either.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Yeah, to be pretty, you'd have to be like that Amy.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Damn it! I thought of her again! How dare she look down on us? They haven't even graduated yet!

blocks.speaker: Rudvans
blocks.content: But what happened with you and Amy? You didn't have to follow her to the skating rink. Those little actions make them look down on us.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Indeed, Jokre had seemed more agitated than usual at the skating rink.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Just before I transferred to Eins, I confessed to Amy.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Oh my, really? So? What happened?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: She said no.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: No? Just no?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Yeah! She just said no! Not 'I'm grateful, but,' not 'You're a good person, but,' not even 'I have someone else I like.' Just no!

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Oh my, that's really mean!

blocks.speaker: Rudvans
blocks.content: Arrogant.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Do you know what the worst part is? I responded with, 'Okay, sorry.' I'll never forget that humiliation.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Pretty people only think about themselves. Hey, hey! It's better this way. Who cares about her? We're mages now. Jokre, you won! Come on, drink.
Jokre downed the drink Vivian poured for him. It burned his throat and felt like fire in his stomach.
But he felt better.
After all, friends were the only ones he could rely on.
Who cared about prestigious schools?
He had found people who understood him, so he had no regrets.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Damn it! Who cares if others get their certificates from guilds? Once we get jobs, we're all mages. And our bosses will be from prestigious schools. Look at Dante. He was beating professional mages even when he was at the Royal Magic School.

blocks.speaker: Rudvans
blocks.content: But that's just gossip from the journal, isn't it?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Who cares? It's all the same. We have to compete with graduates from the kingdom's top five schools. Will major institutions hire us? And we're not even top nobles. We'll end up as lackeys, becoming 9th grade, working like dogs to become 8th grade, and after ten years? The prestigious school graduates will all be 6th grade, sitting in management positions.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: True. And even prestigious schools aren't the end. Those who graduate at the top reach 6th grade in 2-3 years. What are we supposed to do?

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Do you know what's more annoying? Those geniuses become teachers at prestigious schools. It's a cycle of talent. Genius teachers attract genius students. We can't even dream of getting in.
Though Jokre's words were becoming exaggerated, they weren't entirely wrong.
There were exceptions among regular school graduates who reached the top positions in the magical society.
But such anomalies lost their significance against the average.
Regular school graduates were seen as less skilled than those from prestigious schools.
This was the accepted truth.
The pessimistic stories from Jokre and his friends lingered around their drinking spot.
In school, they thought becoming mages would solve everything. But once they became mages, their desperate hopes vanished like a mirage, replaced by new worries.

blocks.speaker: Vivian
blocks.content: Have you ever thought that this world might be a play? We're living our lives, and someone is watching us.
Jokre and Rudvans turned to look at Vivian.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: If that's the case, I would never be the main character. The audience would never want me to succeed. They probably wouldn't even care. They might just want me to exit quickly, or maybe even wish I were dead.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Jokre looked at Vivian with pity.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: She had always been a sensitive friend. But what really hurt was knowing that her words weren't just drunken ramblings.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Maybe she was right.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Why worry about that? Life isn't a play. No one knows what's going to happen. And no one thinks of you that way.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: But think about it. Shirone, Dante, Amy. Do they know how much we want to be like them? They don't. They just stick to their own group. Maybe we never had a place with them, like extras in a play.
The mood at the drinking spot grew extremely somber.
Especially Jokre, who felt pathetic for not being able to refute Vivian's words.

blocks.speaker: Jokre
blocks.content: Damn it! What is this? This is supposed to be a graduation celebration trip! We passed! We became mages! We won! It's just bad luck that we ran into those jerks!
Jokre put the bottle to his lips and drank deeply. The strong liquor burned all the way down his throat.
